tone_and_voice: Board-facing trend tracker -- distinct from Monitoring Report's tactical brevity, Quarterly Performance must situate this quarter within a trajectory (prior quarters, annual trend) for a Board audience tracking programme performance over time.

section_rules.executive_summary: Must explicitly reference the quarter-over-quarter trajectory (e.g. 'continuing a positive trend' or 'a reversal from Q2') -- a quarterly report that reads as a standalone snapshot without trend context has not fulfilled its purpose.
section_rules.discussion: Should cross-reference other recent programme data sources (e.g. routine monitoring touchpoints from the same quarter) to strengthen or caveat confidence in this quarter's findings.
section_rules.methodology: Note the sample size relative to a full annual assessment, since quarterly check-ins are typically lighter-touch and this should be weighted accordingly by the Board.
section_rules.limitations: State explicitly if this quarter's sample size or methodology differs from prior quarters in a way that could affect trend comparability.
section_rules.recommendations: Frame recommendations around whether a persistent finding (present in multiple consecutive quarters) requires escalation to a standing Board risk item, distinct from a single-quarter observation.
section_rules.policy_implications: Rare -- reserve for annual reporting unless a finding has persisted across multiple quarters, indicating a structural rather than transient issue.
section_rules.lessons_learned: Reflect on the value of cross-referencing this quarter's data against other recent programme data sources for confidence calibration.
section_rules.conclusion: State the trend direction clearly and name any item requiring a specific Q+1 resolution update.
section_rules.annexes: Include a simple quarter-over-quarter comparison table for the tracked indicators, since trend visibility is this report type's core value.
section_rules.evidence_style: Always present findings with their trend context (e.g. 'consistent with Q2', 'a new concern not present in Q2') rather than as an isolated point-in-time statistic.

common_kpis: persistence of flagged items across consecutive quarters
common_risks: presenting a quarterly snapshot without trend context, understating or overstating a genuine trajectory
inconsistent sample size or methodology across quarters undermining trend comparability
failing to escalate a finding that has persisted across multiple consecutive quarters
typical_recommendations: request a specific resolution update for any item persisting across multiple quarters
escalate a persistent finding to a standing Board risk item with a named owner
standardize quarterly sample size and question wording for stronger trend comparability
typical_policy_implications: only when a finding has persisted long enough to indicate a structural, not transient, issue
sector_knowledge.typical_donor_expectations: Boards and donors reviewing quarterly reports expect explicit trend framing -- a quarterly report that could just as easily be read as a standalone annual report has not delivered the trend-tracking value this report type exists to provide.

forbidden_behaviors: Never present quarterly findings without explicit reference to the trend trajectory (improving, declining, stable) relative to prior quarters.
Never treat a persistent multi-quarter finding as a fresh observation without acknowledging its recurrence.
Never claim trend comparability across quarters if sample size or methodology changed without flagging this explicitly.
